read.image: crying through the suburbs without him
read.take: She got her license just like they always talked about, ready to drive up to his house, and now drives through tears past it. He is probably with that blonde girl who is everything she is insecure about. The license only circles her back through everything they were. She still fuckin' loves him.
read.contradiction: He has moved on, and she is still driving the route to his door.

arc.label: His Excitement
arc.scene: He was the one who got excited about this, back when her passing the test meant she could finally drive out to where he was. She still heads out with all of that excitement, except now she is the only one who feels it.

arc.label: The Break
arc.scene: On every road he turns up, his face in the white cars, his voice in the traffic. She can't make herself pass the places they used to go. Then the memory she has been fighting wins, the two of them laughing over the noise, back when the drive still had somewhere to go.

arc.label: The Loop
arc.scene: For all of it, she ends up right back where she started, alone on his street. There is no one there to tell, so she says it to the empty road: "You said forever, now I drive alone past your street."

lines.line:
I got my driver's license last week
Just like we always talked about
And you're probably with that blonde girl
I just can't imagine how you could be so okay now that I'm gone
I still fuckin' love you, babe

read.texture: Spare and acoustic, low and quiet, a ballad carried by her voice with almost nothing else in the frame.
